commit.message:
fix(wasm): use viewport size for DPR clamping, not initial canvas size

fit_canvas_to_parent expands canvas to full container width AFTER
start_app(), so using the initial canvas size (276px) gives a wrong
safeDPR. Use window.innerWidth/Height instead — the canvas will never
exceed the viewport.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Per-Book Narration Speed (#167)

* Add auto scroll to ebook reader

# Conflicts:
#	AudioBooth/AudioBooth/Localizable.xcstrings
#	AudioBooth/AudioBooth/Screens/EbookReader/EbookReaderPreferencesView.swift
#	AudioBooth/AudioBooth/Screens/EbookReader/EbookReaderViewModel.swift

* Add per-book narration speed

Store playback speed per book/podcast ID in UserDefaults, falling back
to a configurable default. Add DefaultSpeedPickerSheetViewModel for the
Settings UI. Migrate existing global playbackSpeed on first launch.

* Add default narration speed picker to Player settings

Narration Speed section with Default row that opens FloatPickerSheet.

* Clean up orphaned per-book speed keys

Return orphaned book IDs from MediaProgress.syncFromAPI and remove their
bookSpeed_ keys. Batch-wipe all keys on Clear Persistent Storage.

* Store per-book playback speed in MediaProgress instead of UserDefaults

I moved per-book speed storage from UserDefaults bookSpeed_ keys into
MediaProgress.playbackSpeed (SwiftData). SpeedPickerSheetViewModel now
reads/writes the MediaProgress record directly, so speed gets persisted
alongside all the other progress data.

Also added playbackRate to the API model (User.MediaProgress) so when/if
the ABS server starts returning it, syncFromAPI will pick it up
automatically. Removed the bookSpeed_ orphan cleanup from HomePageModel
and SettingsViewModel since deleting MediaProgress records handles that.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update playing book speed when global default changes

I added an observer on UserPreferences.objectWillChange so the currently
playing book picks up default speed changes from Settings, but only when
the book has no per-book speed set. Consistent with how other player
settings like showFullBookDuration propagate live.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use Stepper for default speed in Player settings

Swapped the FloatPickerSheet for a simple Stepper, per Jeremy's feedback.
The full sheet is overkill for setting a default value. Deleted
DefaultSpeedPickerSheetViewModel since it's no longer needed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Move speed persistence to BookPlayerModel per review feedback

I moved playbackSpeed writes out of SpeedPickerSheetViewModel and into
BookPlayerModel.updateMediaProgress, so speed gets persisted alongside
currentTime and progress in the same save loop. The speed picker now
only handles the init (reading from MediaProgress) and player rate
changes, which is what Jeremy asked for.

The default speed observer also lives in BookPlayerModel now.

* Address review feedback

Reuse playbackSpeed key instead of renaming to defaultPlaybackSpeed,
which drops the migration entirely. Removed API model changes and
syncFromAPI speed logic since the ABS PR might not land as-is. Moved
the default speed observer back into SpeedPickerSheetViewModel per
Jeremy's OK.

* Always write playbackSpeed in updateMediaProgress

Per Jeremy's feedback, just always set mediaProgress.playbackSpeed to
whatever speed the user is listening at. Removes the observer and
applySpeed since we no longer need to distinguish between explicit
per-book speed and inherited default.

---------

Co-authored-by: Jeremy Grenier <1220338+jeremygrenier@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: restrict testcontainers-go to patch updates only in Renovate (#1705)

`testcontainers-go` v0.41.0 raised the minimum Go requirement to 1.25,
conflicting with this project's Go 1.24 floor. Minor/major Renovate
updates to this package must be blocked.

## Changes

- **`.github/renovate.json`**: Added a `packageRule` disabling `minor`
and `major` update types for
`github.com/testcontainers/testcontainers-go`, mirroring the existing
pattern used for `go.opentelemetry.io/**`

```json
{
  "description": "Restrict github.com/testcontainers/testcontainers-go to patch updates only",
  "enabled": false,
  "matchPackageNames": ["github.com/testcontainers/testcontainers-go"],
  "matchUpdateTypes": ["major", "minor"]
}
```

commit.message:
Frontend-driven dimensions: resize command, derived picker row count

The frontend measures its character grid size (cols × rows) using a probe
element and sends it to core. Core uses these dimensions for everything:
viewport, picker result count, pane rects.

Rust backend:
- initial_render(cols, rows): accepts dimensions from frontend on load
- resize(cols, rows): handles window resize, stores dimensions, re-renders
- ViewportSize stored alongside EditorState, used by emit_render
- No more hardcoded 120×40

Frontend:
- measureCharSize(): creates a hidden probe span, measures char width and
  line height from the actual font
- computeGridSize(): derives cols/rows from window.innerWidth/Height
- Window resize debounced at 50ms, sends resize command to Rust
- Initial render sends measured dimensions

Core (render.rs):
- Picker result count derived from render height:
  picker_height = 70% of screen, max_results = picker_height - 4
  (header, input, status, border). Adapts to any window size.
- Replaces hardcoded take(50)

commit.message:
Fix Windows atomic ops to return pre-operation value (match Linux/C11 semantics) (#812)

* Initial plan

* fix: use InterlockedExchangeAdd for Windows atomic ops to return old value

Replace InterlockedAdd/InterlockedIncrement/InterlockedDecrement with
InterlockedExchangeAdd which returns the value before the operation,
matching the behavior of __sync_fetch_and_add (GCC) and
atomic_fetch_add (C11).

Also fix ATOMIC_SUB macro parenthesization: -(n) instead of -n.

Co-authored-by: ithewei <26049660+ithewei@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: prefer GCC builtins over Interlocked for MinGW compatibility

Reorder #ifdef checks so GCC-based compilers (including MinGW) use
__sync_fetch_and_add builtins via a direct __GNUC__ version check,
instead of relying on __GNUC_PREREQ which is broken on MinGW.

Only pure MSVC now uses InterlockedExchangeAdd for atomic operations.
This completely avoids any MinGW compatibility concerns with Windows
Interlocked functions.

Co-authored-by: ithewei <26049660+ithewei@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Android: fix treble clef missing on staff

Android system fonts ship NotoSansSymbols-Regular-Subsetted.ttf which
has the Musical Symbols Unicode block (U+1D11E) stripped out. Neither
Compose TextMeasurer nor native Canvas drawText can render the clef.

Fix: render the clef once on the dev machine (Segoe UI Symbol via
sharp/SVG) and bundle the result as a drawable-nodpi PNG asset.
MusicStaff.kt loads it with BitmapFactory and scales it dynamically
to match the current lineSpacing (clefH = 7 × lineSpacing, aspect-
ratio preserved), so it renders correctly at all screen densities.

Also fix leftMargin: was hardcoded 60px (only ~20dp at xxhdpi);
now lineSpacing × 4.2 so the clef area scales with density.

commit.message:
Fix Directory_Delete_MountVolume test: guard Unmount calls with Directory.Exists check (#125348)

`finally` blocks in Scenarios 1, 2, 3.1, and 3.2 unconditionally called
`MountHelper.Unmount(mountedDirName)`. When `Directory.Delete` removes
the mount point directory as part of the delete operation, the
subsequent `Unmount` call throws (Win32 error 4390/3), gets caught by
the scenario's `catch`, and sets `s_pass = false`.

## Changes

- **`Delete_MountVolume.cs` — Scenarios 1, 2, 3.1, 3.2 `finally`
blocks**: Wrap `Unmount`/`DeleteDir` calls with
`Directory.Exists(mountedDirName)`, matching the pattern already used in
Scenarios 3.3–3.5.

```csharp
// Before
finally
{
    MountHelper.Unmount(mountedDirName);
    DeleteDir(mountedDirName, true);
}

// After
finally
{
    if (Directory.Exists(mountedDirName))
    {
        MountHelper.Unmount(mountedDirName);
        DeleteDir(mountedDirName, true);
    }
}
```

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

## Problem

The `Directory_Delete_MountVolume.RunTest()` test in
`src/libraries/System.Runtime/tests/System.IO.FileSystem.Tests/Directory/Delete_MountVolume.cs`
fails because the `finally` blocks in Scenarios 1, 2, 3.1, and 3.2
unconditionally call `MountHelper.Unmount(mountedDirName)`, but
`Directory.Delete` may have already removed the mount point directory.
When `Unmount` tries to call `DeleteVolumeMountPoint` on a path that no
longer exists, it throws (Win32 error 4390 or 3), which gets caught by
the scenario's `catch` block and sets `s_pass = false`, failing the
test.

Scenarios 3.3, 3.4, and 3.5 already correctly guard their cleanup with
`if (Directory.Exists(mountedDirName))` before attempting to unmount.
The fix is to apply the same pattern to the 4 `finally` blocks that are
missing it.

## Fix

Change the `finally` blocks in Scenarios 1, 2, 3.1, and 3.2 to guard the
`Unmount` and `DeleteDir` calls with `Directory.Exists(mountedDirName)`,
matching the existing pattern used in Scenarios 3.3–3.5.

There are exactly 4 `finally` blocks to change. Each one currently looks
like:

```csharp
finally
{
    MountHelper.Unmount(mountedDirName);
    DeleteDir(mountedDirName, true);
}
```

Each should be changed to:

```csharp
finally
{
    if (Directory.Exists(mountedDirName))
    {
        MountHelper.Unmount(mountedDirName);
        DeleteDir(mountedDirName, true);
    }
}
```

The 4 locations in the file
`src/libraries/System.Runtime/tests/System.IO.FileSystem.Tests/Directory/Delete_MountVolume.cs`
are:

1. **Scenario 1 finally** (around lines 80–84)
2. **Scenario 2 finally** (around lines 125–129)
3. **Scenario 3.1 finally** (around lines 167–171)
4. **Scenario 3.2 finally** (around lines 209–213)

**Do not change anything else.** No changes to `s_pass`, `Eval`, method
signatures, test structure, or any other lines. Only add the
`Directory.Exists` guard to these 4 `finally` blocks.

The following is the prior conversation context from the user's chat
exploration (may be truncated):

User: explain this test to me and what s_pass asserts

Assistant: ## Explanation of `Delete_MountVolume.RunTest()`

This is a Windows-specific test that verifies `Directory.Delete` works
correctly when **NTFS volume mount points** are involved. A volume mount
point is a directory that acts as an entry point to another volume
(similar to a drive letter, but mapped to a folder).

### What It Tests

The test runs **7 scenarios**, all variations of the same theme —
deleting directories that are accessed through or contain mounted
volumes:

| Scenario | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| **1** | A *different* drive is mounted onto the current drive. Delete
a directory on the other drive *via* the mount point. |
| **2** | The *current* drive is mounted onto a different drive. Delete
a directory on the current drive *via* the mount point on the other
drive. |
| **3.1** | The current drive is mounted onto *itself* (at the root).
Delete a directory via that self-referencing mount. |
| **3.2** | The current drive is mounted onto the *current working
directory*. Delete via that mount. |
| **3.3** | ⚠️ Delete the **mounted directory itself** (the mount
point). Verifies the mount point is removed without nuking the
underlying volume. |
| **3.4** | ⚠️ Delete the **parent directory** of a mount point (parent
also has other files/dirs). Verifies recursive delete handles mount
points inside the tree. |
| **3.5** | Same as 3.4 but targets a *different* subdirectory as the
mount point within the tree. |

### The Pattern in Each Scenario

1. **Create** a directory to serve as a mount point.
2. **Mount** a volume onto that directory using `MountHelper.Mount`.
3. **Create** a directory tree (files and subdirectories) on the source
volume.
4. **Delete** the tree — either via the mount path or by deleting a
parent that contains the mount.
5. **Verify** the directory no longer exists.
6. **Clean up** — unmount and delete the mount directory in a `finally`
block.

### What `s_pass` Asserts

`s_pass` is a **boolean accumulator** for the overall test result. It
starts as `true` and is set to `false` in two situations:

1. **Any `Eval(...)` call returns `false`** — `Eval` checks a condition
(e.g., "directory was deleted") and sets `s_pass = false` if the
condition fails, logging the error.
2. **Any scenario throws an unhandled exception** — each `catch` block
sets `s_pass = false` and logs the error.

At the very end, in the outermost `finally`:

```csharp
Assert.True(s_pass);
```

This is a **single assertion that summarizes all scenarios**. If *any*
scenario failed a condition or threw an exception, `s_pass` will be
`false` and the test fails. This pattern was common in older test code —
it lets the test run all scenarios even if one fails (rather than
stopping at the first `Assert` failure), collecting all errors before
reporting.

### Why It's Disabled

The `[ActiveIssue("https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/14378")]`
attribute means this...

</details>

fix: permission revert on WS  dedup + retry unconfirmedreconnect

 WS drops before server
 reconnect replays from DB (no permission_response
 permission shows buttons again, requiring a second Allow.

Fixes:
1. Live permission_request dedup: skip if element with same requestId
   already exists in DOM (handles bridge restore re-emission)
2. Track unconfirmed responses: state.unconfirmedPermissions Map records
   responses sent but not yet confirmed by server (permission_resolved)
3. Retry on reconnect: retryUnconfirmedPermissions() resends pending
   responses after loadNewEvents, re-applying optimistic UI

7 new tests covering dedup, tracking, retry, skip-resolved, cleanup.
331 unit + 48 E2E pass.

commit.message:
Add vectorEmbeddingPolicy support to Spark catalog container creation (#48349)

* Add vectorEmbeddingPolicy support to Spark catalog container creation

Enable configuring Vector Embedding Policy via TBLPROPERTIES when creating
containers through the Spark catalog API. This allows users to set up
vector-search-enabled containers directly from Spark SQL.

Changes:
- Add vectorEmbeddingPolicy TBLPROPERTIES key to CosmosContainerProperties
- Add JSON serialization bridge methods in SparkModelBridgeInternal
- Apply vector embedding policy in both CosmosCatalogCosmosSDKClient and
  CosmosCatalogManagementSDKClient container creation paths
- Expose vector embedding policy in container metadata readback
- Add VectorEmbeddingPolicy constant to CosmosConstants.TableProperties
- Add integration test for container creation with vector embedding policy
- Update existing tblProperties size assertions to account for new property

Usage example:
  CREATE TABLE catalog.db.container (...) USING cosmos.oltp
    TBLPROPERTIES(
      partitionKeyPath = '/mypk',
      indexingPolicy = '{..."vectorIndexes":[{"path":"/v1","type":"flat"}]}',
      vectorEmbeddingPolicy = '{"vectorEmbeddings":[{"path":"/v1","dataType":"float32","distanceFunction":"cosine","dimensions":1536}]}'
    )
